# [OS-02] Reproducible `.t27 → RTL → shuttle` flow

> Plan ID `OS-02` — local plan identifier in this issue pack, **not** a
> GitHub issue number. See [`00_EPIC_2026.md`](00_EPIC_2026.md).

## Status

✅ **Done.** The numeric-format registry under `specs/numeric/*.t27`
is mirrored from the [`t27`](https://github.com/gHashTag/t27)
toolchain. CI workflows exercise the flow on every push.

## Evidence in this repo

- Format registry: `specs/numeric/*.t27` (gf4..gf256, fp8 e4m3 /
  e5m2, posit16, nf4, int4 / int8, GoldenFloat family).
- Reference: [`docs/TRI_NET_FORMATS_SUMMARY.md`](../../docs/TRI_NET_FORMATS_SUMMARY.md).
- CI: `.github/workflows/test.yaml` (iverilog + R-SI-1 + cocotb).
- CI: `.github/workflows/gds.yaml` (OpenLane2 / SKY130A).
- External: [`t27`](https://github.com/gHashTag/t27) — toolchain repo.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Format registry present in this tree and mirrored from t27.
- [x] CI gates the flow on every push.
- [x] No format claimed in `specs/numeric/` that is not synthesisable
      at SKY130A.

## R5 honesty

- This issue does **not** claim that every `.t27` specification has
  matched RTL in `src/`. Some entries (e.g. exotic GF widths) are
  registry placeholders; the synthesisable subset is what is in
  `info.yaml`.
- The toolchain repo (`t27`) is the authoritative source for the
  `.t27 → RTL` lowering rules; this repo is a consumer.
